summary: Inserting citations in LibreOffice Writer does not work when there are unaccpeted track changes

description.text:
### JabRef version

6.0-alpha2

### Operating system

Windows

### Details on version and operating system

_No response_

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

I´m using Libreoffice Writer (Version: 25.2.3.2 (x86)) on Windows and I was using JabRef 5.15 Because of a bug I switched to the alpha version, but the bug is also there, however a bit different:

1) In JabRef 5.15 I connect to an open LibreOffice document. When inserting citations I´m getting the error message:

`Changes by JabRef could result in unexpected interactions with recorded changes.
Use [Edit]/[Track Changes]/[Manage] to resolve them first.`

This does not show up when there are no tracked changes or when the changes have been accepted. 

2) In Version JabRef 6.0-alpha2.836--2025-10-02--883b27d adding citations is basically working, but making / syncing the bibliography fails with the same error message sadly. 

Is there any workaround for this?





### Appendix

<summary>Log File</summary>

```
Paste an excerpt of your log file here
```

</details>

summary: Add search and book support to EuropePmcFetcher

description.text:
- Implements SearchBasedParserFetcher to allow for keyword searches.
- Updates the parser to handle multiple results from the API.
- Adds logic to correctly identify and parse 'Book' entry types.

Fixes #13400

Closes #13400

This pull request extends the `EuropePmcFetcher` to support keyword-based searches, in addition to the existing ID-based fetching. It also adds the ability to correctly identify and parse 'Book' entry types from the EuropePMC API response, fulfilling the feature request in issue #13400.

### Steps to test

1.  Go to **Library** -> **Web search**.
2.  Select `EuropePMC` from the list of fetchers.
3.  In the search bar, type a query like `COVID-19 vaccine` and click **Search**.
4.  **Verify that a list of articles appears.** Select one and ensure it imports correctly.
5.  Next, test the ID-based fetcher by going to **Library** -> **New entry**.
6.  In the "ID-based entry generator" section, select `EuropePMC` as the ID type.
7.  Enter the ID `33235449` and click **Generate**.
8.  **Verify that a single, complete entry is created and added to your library.**

### Mandatory checks

I removed `--bind-services` - maybe, it works.

summary: Unintentional reset of search query during manipulation of found entries.

description.text:
When you perform a search, a list of found entries is displayed. After **deleting** an entry from the list, the search query is completely reset to the main window displaying the entries before the search, even though the search query is still entered in the search bar. Therefore, to display the search query, you must clear the search bar again and re-enter the same query. This issue wasn't previously present; it arose relatively recently.

If the list resulting from a search query is manipulated in any way, the search query should not be reset and the resulting list should remain until the search query is removed or modified!

summary: Make Web Search 'Configure API key' searchable in preferences #13929

description.text:
Closes #13929

This PR adds the ability to search for the "Configure API key" option in the Web Search preferences tab

### Changes
- Updated `WebSearchTab.java` to add search keywords such as "Configure API key", "Custom API key", "api", "api key", "apikey"
- Updated `PreferencesTab.java` to include a default `getSearchKeywords()` method
- Modified `PreferencesSearchHandler.java` to consider keywords when filtering tabs

With this change, users can quickly find the "Configure API key" option by typing relevant search terms in the preferences search bar

### Steps to Test
1. Open JabRef
2. Go to **Preferences → Web Search** tab
3. In the search bar at the top of Preferences, type any of the following:
   - "Configure API key"
   - "Custom API key"
   - "api"
   - "api key"
4. Verify that the Web Search tab is highlighted as a search result

### Visual Reference
![Web Search Preferences](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/13e5b5ba-3e7f-4957-bec0-fc91950f5efa)

### Mandatory checks
- [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the MIT license
- [x] I manually tested my changes in running JabRef
- [x] I described the change in `CHANGELOG.md`
- [x] I added screenshots in the PR description (if change is visible to the user)
- [ ] I added JUnit tests for changes (if applicable)
- [ ] I checked the user documentation: Is the information available and up to date?

- Updated About dialog: "Get Involved" link now points to my fork.
- Added "SE" button in MainToolBar that opens the Software Engineering course page.

summary: Duplicated entry removed from main table when merged

description.text:
### JabRef version

Latest development branch build (please note build date below)

### Operating system

GNU / Linux

### Details on version and operating system

Arch Linux, bspwm, X11

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1. Create a new library.
2. Import a new article by ID (e.g. <https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.02318>).
3. Then, import the same article again.
4. Then a merge dialog will be opened, because the new entry duplicates the one already present (this is expected behavior).
5. Click on "Treat all duplicates the same way".
6. Click on "Keep merged".

As the result, main table no longer has the entry and it's empty. However, the source contains the entry, and if you reopen it, it will be seen

### Appendix

_No response_

summary: Add options to choose groups during import

description.text: Closes #9191 This PR introduce options to choose groups from library during import of the entry(s). * Default group will be active group (opened by user). * On selecting the library, last selected group will be shown first (from the list). * Groups are alphabetically sorted Potential future work (needs to create ticket, not in this PR): Add option to create simple group from same importDialog (sometimes user may found articles that needs to be deal in seperate group). ### Steps to test 1. Create a new bib file 2. Open the groups pane 3. Add new group "test" (using context menu) 4. Open web search (View menu) 5. Select "DBLP" 6. Search for "Using static analysis to find bugs" 7. summary: Part 3/5: add Merge Bookkeeper to record GUI-applied semantic merges

description.text:
Closes #12350 

Introduce the Merge Bookkeeper component that writes the result of a GUI-applied semantic merge back into Git as the correct commit shape (fast-forward / single-parent commit / merge commit). This is the third step after SemanticMergeAnalyzer (compute) and GUI application (apply).


Follow-ups (separate PRs)
- Migrate test classes and add assertions to verify the contents of autoPlan and conflicts.
- After the above, do the final cleanup and wiring.

summary: Part 2/5: wire SemanticMergeAnalyzer into sync flow

description.text:
Closes #12350 

Wire SemanticMergeAnalyzer into the Git sync path so pulling remote changes no longer writes directly to the DB and therefore does not trigger the “The library has been modified by another program.” dialog. The analyzer now produces conflicts + autoPlan, and the GUI layer remains responsible for applying them.

Follow-ups (next PRs)
- Merge Bookkeeper (git bookkeeping).
- Migrate test classes and add assertions to verify the contents of autoPlan and conflicts.
- After the above, do the final cleanup and wiring.

Apologies for the line count. A lot of the changes here are identical to those in [#13970](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/13970); I had to bring them back to make the project compile. 

### Steps to test

- Use “Share to GitHub” to create a remote BibTeX repository
- Modify the file on the remote side
- In JabRef, go to File → Git → Pull
- Verify that the GUI no longer shows file being externally altered warnings
